seoTitle: Remove Auto Add Rules in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to find and remove auto add rules in QuickBooks Online to keep your transaction management accurate and under control.
intro: Auto add rules can save time by automating repetitive transaction categorization, but sometimes a rule needs to be removed or adjusted. This guide shows how to locate and remove auto add rules in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Access the auto add rules settings
steps.body: Navigate to the banking or rules section of QuickBooks Online where existing automation rules are listed.

steps.heading: Locate the rule to remove
steps.body: Find the specific auto add rule that's no longer needed or is causing incorrect categorization of transactions.

steps.heading: Remove the rule
steps.body: Delete the rule so it no longer automatically applies to incoming transactions going forward.

steps.heading: Review recent transactions
steps.body: Check recently categorized transactions to see if any need correcting after the rule has been removed.

faq.question: Will removing a rule affect past transactions?
faq.answer: No, removing an auto add rule only stops it from applying to future transactions; past categorizations remain unless manually changed.

faq.question: Why would I need to remove an auto add rule?
faq.answer: Rules may need removal if they're miscategorizing transactions, are no longer relevant, or are causing confusion in your books.

faq.question: Can I edit a rule instead of deleting it?
faq.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online allows you to edit existing rules if you just need to adjust the criteria rather than remove it entirely.

faq.question: Are auto add rules available on all plans?
faq.answer: This automation feature is generally available across QuickBooks Online plans, though options may vary slightly by subscription tier.
